channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Longtime Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100
item.description: Alan Greenspan, who steered the Federal Reserve for nearly nearly two decades through some of the longest economic booms in U.S. history, has died.
item.content:encoded: Alan Greenspan, who steered the Federal Reserve for nearly nearly two decades through some of the longest economic booms in U.S. history, has died.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Congress returns as scrutiny grows over Trump's Iran ceasefire agreement
item.description: With Congress returning to Washington, President Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz faces new scrutiny.
item.content:encoded: With Congress returning to Washington, President Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz faces new scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: Rep. Adam Smith, D-Wash., on where talks between the U.S. and Iran stand
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks the Democratic leader of the House Armed Services Committee, Rep. Adam Smith of Washington state, about the opening of face-to-face talks between the U.S. and Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks the Democratic leader of the House Armed Services Committee, Rep. Adam Smith of Washington state, about the opening of face-to-face talks between the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Former Vice President Mike Pence talks about his book, 'What Conservatives Believe'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Vice President Mike Pence about his new book, "What Conservatives Believe."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Vice President Mike Pence about his new book, "What Conservatives Believe."
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer steps down, Vance and senior Iranian officials meet in Switzerland for high-stakes nuclear talks, Trump's Iran ceasefire faces new questions as Congress returns.
item.content:encoded: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer steps down, Vance and senior Iranian officials meet in Switzerland for high-stakes nuclear talks, Trump's Iran ceasefire faces new questions as Congress returns.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
item.description: The U.K. will have its seventh prime minister in 10 years. Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor is the outgoing Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham.
item.content:encoded: The U.K. will have its seventh prime minister in 10 years. Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor is the outgoing Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Despite state bans, abortions have almost doubled in the U.S.
item.description: Four years ago, the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade. Yet there are nearly twice as many abortions in the U.S. as before. That's because abortion pills are now being prescribed online and mailed.
item.content:encoded: Four years ago, the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade. Yet there are nearly twice as many abortions in the U.S. as before. That's because abortion pills are now being prescribed online and mailed.
item.dc:creator: Kate Wells

item.title: Prison tattoo program gives participants skills while reducing risk
item.description: A new program in Missouri allows people serving time in prison to earn a tattoo license while also reducing the risk of infections and illness from contraband tattoos.
item.content:encoded: A new program in Missouri allows people serving time in prison to earn a tattoo license while also reducing the risk of infections and illness from contraband tattoos.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca A Smith

item.title: Expanded World Cup delivers despite early doubts
item.description: The group stage of the World Cup continues this week. The U.S. has already won its group and qualified for the knockout stage. A few favorites have struggled and unexpected ones have shined.
item.content:encoded: The group stage of the World Cup continues this week. The U.S. has already won its group and qualified for the knockout stage. A few favorites have struggled and unexpected ones have shined.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Ukrainian soldiers march in Kyiv's Pride parade to push for equal rights
item.description: Ukrainian soldiers and their partners march in Kyiv's Pride parade for the right to marry and enter into civil partnerships in a traditional country that's trying to modernize as it aligns with Western Europe.
item.content:encoded: Ukrainian soldiers and their partners march in Kyiv's Pride parade for the right to marry and enter into civil partnerships in a traditional country that's trying to modernize as it aligns with Western Europe.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis
